project_type: Vanilla JavaScript PWA with Firebase Firestore (client-side SDK), deployed on Netlify (static hosting)
language: JavaScript (ES6, no bundler, no modules - global script loading)
build_system: No build system. Static files served directly. ESLint + Prettier for linting/formatting.
test_framework: No unit test framework. Validation via eslint only.
test_command: npx prettier --check core.js sw.js index.html manifest.json
verification_instructions: 1. Run eslint on modified JS files. 2. Run prettier check. 3. Manually verify Firebase SDK script tags load correctly. 4. Verify service worker file is valid JS.
environment_constraints: No server-side runtime for the app itself. Firebase Cloud Functions code will be in a separate functions/ directory that gets deployed separately. The main app is purely client-side static files.
contribution_requirements: Code must pass eslint and prettier checks. Use single quotes, semicolons, 2-space indent.
key_patterns: Firebase compat SDK v10.12.2 loaded via CDN script tags. All JS files use global scope (no modules). Service worker at /sw.js with cache-first strategy. Firebase config in core.js line 7-14. Login via doLogin() stores session in localStorage. renderApp() calls listenNotifications() for real-time Firestore listeners. Notification sound uses programmatic WAV generation. currentUser object has id, nama, role, departemen fields.
relevant_files: core.js - main app logic, notification system (lines 519-780), login (line 77), renderApp (line 133), SW registration (line 872-878)
sw.js - basic service worker, needs push event handler
index.html - loads Firebase SDK scripts (lines 892-893), loads all JS modules
manifest.json - PWA manifest, needs gcm_sender_id for FCM
netlify.toml - deployment config, may need headers for firebase-messaging-sw.js
directory_structure: Flat structure at root: index.html, core.js, sw.js, modules-*.js, manifest.json, netlify.toml. No src/ directory. Firebase Cloud Functions will go in functions/ subdirectory.
